---
title: "FFCWS Data Cleaning Secondary School Data"
output:
  word_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```
# SETUP

## load libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(stargazer)
library(mice)
library(sjPlot)
library(labelled)
library(psych)
```

## load data
```{r}
start_time <- Sys.time()

# load dataset
df_raw <- haven::read_dta("ffcws.dta")

# calc time to load dataset
end_time <- Sys.time()
end_time - start_time

# Convert all column names to lowercase
names(df_raw) <- tolower(names(df_raw))
```

## replace missing values
```{r}
df_na <- df_raw

start_time <- Sys.time()
df_na[df_na==-9] <- NA # not in wave
df_na[df_na==-8] <- NA # out of range
df_na[df_na==-7] <- NA # n/a
df_na[df_na==-6] <- NA # skip
df_na[df_na==-5] <- NA # not asked
df_na[df_na==-4] <- NA # multiple answer
df_na[df_na==-3] <- NA # missing
df_na[df_na==-2] <- NA # don't know
df_na[df_na==-1] <- NA # refuse
end_time <- Sys.time()
end_time - start_time


df_na = df_na %>% drop_unused_value_labels()
#see new values
# view_df(df_na, show.values = TRUE, show.prc = TRUE, show.na = TRUE, sort.by.name = TRUE)
```


## rename variables
```{r}
df_na <- df_na %>%
  dplyr::rename(
  # Affective Control - Depressive Symptoms
    blues              = k6d2c,
    sad                = k6d2n,
    happy              = k6d2s,
    life               = k6d2x,
    dep                = k6d2ac,
  # Controls - Prior Achievement
    grades22           = k7b13,
    wj10               = ch5wj10ss,
  
  ## Year 5 Teacher Survey
  y5_skills_language = t4a5,
  y5_skills_science = t4a6,
  y5_skills_math = t4a7,
  y5_limit_misbehavior = t4f1a,
  y5_limit_kidscantlearn = t4f1b,
  y5_limit_admin = t4f1c,
  y5_enjoy = t4f1d,
  y5_diference = t4f1e,
  y5_chooseteaching = t4f1f,
  y5_influence = t4f2,
  y5_chooseskills = t4f3,
  ## Year 9 Teacher Survey
  
  
  ## math classes
    math_basic = k7b14_1  ,
    math_alg1 =k7b14_2  ,
    math_geom =k7b14_3  ,
    math_alg2 =k7b14_4  ,
    math_trig =k7b14_5  ,
    math_precal = k7b14_6  ,
    math_calc = k7b14_7  ,
    math_adv = k7b14_8  ,
    math_other = k7b14_9  ,
    math_none = k7b14_10 ,
    math_ap = k7b23_1  ,
  ## science classes
    science_bio = k7b15_1,
    science_chem = k7b15_2,
    science_phys = k7b15_3,
    science_other = k7b15_4,
    science_none = k7b15_5,
    science_ap = k7b23_2,
  ## honors/advanced classes
    honors_student = k6b18,
    honors_count = k7b21,
    ap_count = k7b22,
    english_ap = k7b23_3,
    social_ap = k7b23_4,
  # college related
    took_sat = k7b18a, 
    took_act = k7b18b, 
    score_sat = k7b19,
    score_act = k7b20,
    current_college = k7b45, 
    ever_college = k7b45,
    highest_edu = k7b1, 
  # Kind of school
    kind_school        =  k7b7,
      ## expected graduation rates
    grad_high_class = k6b16, 
    grad_coll_class = k6b17,
    grad_high_friend = k6b14,
    grad_coll_friend = k6b15,  
    grad_coll_self = k6b11,
  # Demographic Characteristics
    y15_age            = ck6yagey,
    mom_edu            = cm1edu,
    black             =  k7h1_2,
    white            = k7h1_1,
    aapi = k7h1_3,
    indigenous = k7h1_4,
    another_race = k7h1_5,
    race_ethnicity     = ck6ethrace,
    y15_pov            = cp6povco,
  )

# change ungraded to NA in the grades answer
df_na$grades22[df_na$grades22 == 9] <- NA
```

## recode binary variables and reverse college variables
```{r}
# for most (except biological sex) 1 is yes and 2 is no, so ifelse works
df_na <- df_na %>% dplyr::mutate(
  
  # school programming
  esl = ifelse(p6c18 == 1,1,0),
  sped = ifelse(p6c20 == 1,1,0),
  gt = ifelse(p6c19 == 1,1,0),
  
  # advanced coursework
  honors = ifelse(honors_student == 1, 1, 0),
  ap = ifelse(ap_count == 0, 0, 1),
  ap_math = case_when(
    math_ap == 1 ~ 1,
    ap == 0 | math_ap == 0 ~ 0
  ),
  ap_science = case_when(
    science_ap == 1 ~ 1,
    ap == 0 | science_ap == 0 ~ 0
  ),
    ap_english = case_when(
    english_ap == 1 ~ 1,
    ap == 0 | english_ap == 0 ~ 0
  ),
  ap_social = case_when(
    social_ap == 1 ~ 1,
    ap == 0 | social_ap == 0 ~ 0
  ),
  
  # college
  sat = ifelse(took_sat == 1, 1, 0),
  act = ifelse(took_act == 1, 1, 0),
  ever_college_enroll = ifelse(ever_college == 1, 1, 0),
  
  # demographics
  female = ifelse(cm1bsex == 2,1,0),
  mom_US = ifelse(m1h2 == 1,1,0),
  mom_married = ifelse(m1b2 == 1,1,0),
  frpl = ifelse(p6j35 == 1, 1, 0),
  latinx = ifelse(k7h2 == 1, 1, 0),
  
  # school settings
  popo_stationed = ifelse(k6b2 == 1,1,0),
  popo_see_stop = ifelse(k6e15 == 1,1,0),
  security_stationed = ifelse(k6b3 == 1,1,0),
  suspension_expulsion = ifelse(k6b29 == 1,1,0),
  oss = ifelse(k7b35a == 1, 1, 0),
  iss = ifelse(k7b35b == 1, 1, 0),
  public_school = ifelse(kind_school == 1, 1, 0),
  
  # Parent & student Expectations
  parent_college_importance   = (4-k6b10), # reverse scale 1 very important to 3
  student_college_importance  = (4-k6b9), # reverse scale 1 very important to 3
  parent_college_likelihood   = (5-p6c27), # reverse scale 1 very likely to 4
  student_college_likelihood  = (5-grad_coll_self), # reverse scale 1 very likely to 4
  )
                    
```
## create new math and race variables
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(haven)

df_na <- df_na %>%
  mutate(
    # ------------------
    # Race (character)
    # ------------------
    race = case_when(
      black == 1          ~ "Black",
      another_race == 1   ~ "Another race",
      white == 1          ~ "White",
      indigenous == 1     ~ "Indigenous",
      aapi == 1           ~ "AAPI",
      TRUE                ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # ------------------
    # High school math (6-level)
    # ------------------
    high_math = case_when(
      is.na(math_none) ~ NA_real_,

      math_calc == 1 | math_ap == 1 ~ 5,
      math_precal == 1 | math_trig == 1 | math_adv == 1 ~ 4,
      math_alg2 == 1 ~ 3,
      math_geom == 1 ~ 2,
      math_alg1 == 1 ~ 1,
      math_basic == 1 | math_other == 1 | math_none == 1 ~ 0
    ),
  math_credits = math_alg1 + math_geom + math_alg2 + math_precal + math_calc,
    high_math = labelled(
      high_math,
      labels = c(
        "none or basic" = 0,
        "alg1" = 1,
        "geometry" = 2,
        "alg2" = 3,
        "trig, precalc/alg3 or other advanced math" = 4,
        "calc or AP math" = 5
      )
    ),

    # ------------------
    # Collapsed math level (4-level)
    # ------------------
    level_math = case_when(
      is.na(math_none) ~ NA_real_,

      math_calc == 1 | math_ap == 1 ~ 4,
      math_precal == 1 | math_trig == 1 | math_adv == 1 ~ 3,
      math_alg2 == 1 ~ 2,
      math_geom == 1 | math_alg1 == 1 | math_basic == 1 |
        math_other == 1 | math_none == 1 ~ 1
    ),

    level_math = labelled(
      level_math,
      labels = c(
        "geometry or below" = 1,
        "alg2" = 2,
        "trig, precalc/alg3 or other advanced math" = 3,
        "calc or AP math" = 4
      )
    ),

    # ------------------
    # High school science
    # ------------------
    high_science = case_when(
      is.na(science_none) ~ NA_real_,

      science_ap == 1   ~ 4,
      science_phys == 1 ~ 3,
      science_chem == 1 ~ 2,
      science_bio == 1  ~ 1,
      science_other == 1 | science_none == 1 ~ 0
    ),

    high_science = labelled(
      high_science,
      labels = c(
        "none or other" = 0,
        "biology" = 1,
        "chemistry" = 2,
        "physics" = 3,
        "AP science" = 4
      )
    )
  )

# ------------------
# AP indicators
# ------------------
df_na <- df_na %>%
  mutate(
    ap_stem = case_when(
      is.na(math_none) ~ NA_real_,
      science_ap == 1 | math_ap == 1 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    ap_math = case_when(
      is.na(math_none) ~ NA_real_,
      math_ap == 1 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    ap_science = case_when(
      is.na(science_none) ~ NA_real_,
      science_ap == 1 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    )
  )

# ------------------
# Advanced math dummy
# ------------------
df_na <- df_na %>%
  mutate(adv_math = if_else(high_math >= 5, 1, 0))

# ------------------
# Effect coding (White = reference)
# race_ethnicity: 1 = White, 2 = Black, 3 = Latino, 4 = Another Race, 5 = Multiracial
# ------------------
df_na <- df_na %>%
  mutate(
    black_ec = case_when(
      race_ethnicity == 2 ~  1,
      race_ethnicity == 1 ~ -1,
      TRUE                ~  0
    ),
    latinx_ec = case_when(
      race_ethnicity == 3 ~  1,
      race_ethnicity == 1 ~ -1,
      TRUE                ~  0
    ),
    another_ec = case_when(
      race_ethnicity == 4 ~  1,
      race_ethnicity == 1 ~ -1,
      TRUE                ~  0
    ),
    multi_ec = case_when(
      race_ethnicity == 5 ~  1,
      race_ethnicity == 1 ~ -1,
      TRUE                ~  0
    )
  )
  
```

# Create Validated Scales
```{r}
df_na <- df_na %>%
  mutate(
    # ---- CES-D Depression scale ----
    blues2 = case_when(
      blues == 4 ~ 0,
      blues == 3 ~ 1,
      blues == 2 ~ 2,
      blues == 1 ~ 3),
    sad2 = case_when(
      sad == 4 ~ 0,
      sad == 3 ~ 1,
      sad == 2 ~ 2,
      sad == 1 ~ 3),
    dep2 = case_when(
      dep == 4 ~ 0,
      dep == 3 ~ 1,
      dep == 2 ~ 2,
      dep == 1 ~ 3),
    life2 = case_when(
      life == 4 ~ 0,
      life == 3 ~ 1,
      life == 2 ~ 2,
      life == 1 ~ 3),
    happy2 = case_when(
      happy == 4 ~ 3,
      happy == 3 ~ 2,
      happy == 2 ~ 2,
      happy == 1 ~ 0),
    ces_d = (blues2 + sad2 + dep2 + life2 +  happy2),
    scale_depressive = as.numeric(scale(ces_d)),
    mean_depressive = (blues2 + sad2 + dep2 + life2 +  happy2)/5)
```
## Year 3
```{r}
df_na <- df_na %>% 
  mutate(
    asbi_scale_y03 = rowMeans(select(., p3m4, p3m8, p3m12, p3m15, p3m20, p3m24, p3m27, p3m34, p3m43), na.rm = TRUE),
    asbi_scale_y05 = rowMeans(select(., p4l3, p4l11, p4l15, p4l30, p4l32, p4l41, p4l48, p4l51, p4l55, p4l58, p4l60, p4l63, p4l66), na.rm = TRUE),
    asbi_scale_y09 = rowMeans(select(., p5q3dc:p5q3do), na.rm = TRUE),
    asbi_scale_y15 = rowMeans(select(., k6d1a:k6d1l), na.rm = TRUE),
    # using the constructed variable makes more sense here since the questions are different depending on the city. 
    mom_lib_dep_y01 = cm2md_case_lib,
    dad_lib_dep_y01 = cf2md_case_lib,
    mom_con_dep_y01 = cm2md_case_con,
    dad_con_dep_y01 = cf2md_case_con,
    mom_lib_dep_y03 = cm3md_case_lib,
    dad_lib_dep_y03 = cf3md_case_lib,
    mom_con_dep_y03 = cm3md_case_con,
    dad_con_dep_y03 = cf3md_case_con,
    mom_lib_dep_y05 = cm4md_case_lib,
    dad_lib_dep_y05 = cf4md_case_lib,
    mom_con_dep_y05 = cm4md_case_con,
    dad_con_dep_y05 = cf4md_case_con,
    mom_lib_dep_y09 = cm5md_case_lib,
    dad_lib_dep_y09 = cf5md_case_lib,
    mom_con_dep_y09 = cm5md_case_con,
    dad_con_dep_y09 = cf5md_case_con,
    # this is when they switch to parental caregiver instead of dad and mom
    pcg_lib_dep_y15 = cp5md_case_lib,
    pcg_lib_dep_y15 = cp5md_case_lib,
    pcg_lib_dep_y22 = cp7md_case_lib,
    pcg_lib_dep_y22 = cp7md_case_lib,
    # Neihborhood efficacy - note these are not validated/standardized because edited from original measures; lower scores indicate higher levels of collective efficacy
    pcg_hood_eff_y03 = rowMeans(select(., p3k1a:p3k1e, p3k2a-p3k2e, p3k3a:p3k3h), na.rm = TRUE),
    mom_hood_eff_y05 = rowMeans(select(., m4i0m1:m4i0m5, m4i0n1:m4i0n5), na.rm = TRUE),
    dad_hood_eff_y05 = rowMeans(select(., d4i0m1:d4i0m5, d4i0n1:d4i0n5), na.rm = TRUE),
    # year nine reverse code p4m3c and p5m3d
    p5m3c = 5 - p4m3c, p5m3d = 5 - p5m3d,
    pcg_hood_eff_y09 = rowMeans(select(., p5m2a:p5m2d, p5m3a:p5m3d), na.rm = TRUE),
    # year 15 reverse code p6i9/k6e2c and p6i10/k6e2d 
    p6i9 = 5 - p6i9, k6e2c = 5 - k6e2c,
    p6i10 = 5 - p6i10, k6e2d = 5 - k6e2d,
    pcg_hood_eff_y15= rowMeans(select(., p6i2:p6i10), na.rm = TRUE),
    kid_hood_eff_y15= rowMeans(select(.,k6e2a:k6e2d, k6e3a:k6e3d, na.rm = TRUE)),
    
    # Home observational scale,note says item scores are reversed to that higher score represents a better situation excerpt for condition of surround block (4 point) all others are 2 points (0,1s). reverse codeing them individually would be a pain so if I have the max sum I can add it in front to just reverse it all at once.
    
    responsivity_home_y03 = rowSums(select(., o3t1:o3t9), na.rm = TRUE),
    acceptance_home_y03 = rowSums(select(., o3t10:o3t14), na.rm = TRUE),
    involve_home_y03 = rowSums(select(., o3t15, o3t14), na.rm = TRUE),
    interior_home_y03 = rowSums(select(., o3r1:o3r13), na.rm = TRUE),
    surrounding_home_y03 = rowSums(select(., o3p1:o3p5), na.rm = TRUE),
    exterior_home_y03 = rowSums(select(., o3p6_a:o3p7_c), na.rm = TRUE),
    home_scale_y03 = rowSums(select(., ends_with("home_y03")), na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # need to get a copy of the rating scale. will request.
    ecers = rowMeans(select(., e3f1:e3f7, e3i1:e3i38, e3l1:e3l11), na.rm = TRUE))

# note, need to do the home observation scale for year 5, but they direct to hte Leventhall et al. paper instead of explaining how to construcgt the scale. note that year 5 also has parental versions of the scale ase well
    
```


# Create Y15 Scales
```{r}
df_na <- df_na %>%
  mutate(    
      # Teacher Expectations
    teacher_care           = 5 - k6b4a,
    teacher_respect        = 5 - k6b4b,
    teacher_expectation    = 5 - k6b4c,
    teacher_interesting    = 5 - k6b4d,
    teacher_comprehensible = 5 - k6b4e,
    teacher_learn          = 5 - k6b4f,
    teacher_busy           = 5 - k6b4g,
  # Classmate expectations
    kids_respect_teachers = 5 - k6b5a,
    kids_work_hard = 5 - k6b5b,
    kids_behave = 5 - k6b5c,
  # Outcome - School Belonging
    school_close       = 5 - k6b1a,
    school_part        = 5 - k6b1b,
    school_happy       = 5 - k6b1c,
    school_safe        = 5 - k6b1d,
    # ---- Teacher perception scale ----
   ## add all reverse scaled items
   teacher_scale_raw = 
     teacher_care  +        
     teacher_respect   +    
     teacher_expectation +  
     teacher_interesting   +
     teacher_comprehensible +
     teacher_learn +        
     teacher_busy,          

    scale_teacher = as.numeric(scale(teacher_scale_raw)),
    mean_teacher = teacher_scale_raw/7,

    # ---- School belonging scale ----
    school_belonging_scale_raw =
    school_close +
    school_part +
    school_happy +
    school_safe,
    scale_belonging = as.numeric(scale(school_belonging_scale_raw)),
    mean_belonging = school_belonging_scale_raw/4
  )
```

## check scale reliability with original data
```{r}
# install to get the greatest lower bound for reliability
##install.packages("Rcsdp")

# Check reliability
## depressive symptoms
dep_scale <- df_na %>% dplyr::select(blues2, sad2, happy2, life2, dep2)
reliability(dep_scale)

## teacher perceptions
teacher_scale <- df_na %>% dplyr::select(teacher_care, teacher_respect, teacher_expectation, teacher_interesting, teacher_comprehensible)
reliability(teacher_scale)

# school belonging
belonging_scale <- df_na %>% dplyr::select(school_close, school_part, school_happy, school_safe)
reliability(belonging_scale)

# kindergarten scales
ece_teach <- df_na %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("t5f"))
reliability(ece_teach)

```


# Save dataset
```{r}
haven::write_dta(df_na, "ffcws_subset.dta")
```
